title: Dashboard 'next rebalance' countdown is calendar-naive (counts to next UTC midnight, ignores weekend/market closure)
source: CEO observation 2026-05-30 (Sat 00:0X UTC): dashboard showed 'next rebalance in 0h52m ~ 00:00 UTC' but no rebalance fired — diagnosed as FX weekend (no new EURUSD D1 bar). The countdown is misleading on weekends.
why: webapp/templates/dashboard.html:259-263 computes next rebalance as Date.UTC(...getUTCDate()+1,0,0,0) = next UTC midnight, unconditionally. It does not model the market calendar: the daily rebalance fires on a NEW closed D1 bar of the cursor-driving symbol (EURUSD, universe[0]), which has no weekend bar. So on Fri/Sat/Sun the countdown counts down to a midnight at which nothing happens.
value_rationale: Medium: cosmetic but actively misleading during the live competition (the 5-day window includes a weekend). A trader watching the dashboard would expect a rebalance that never comes.
scope: Compute next-rebalance from the actual market calendar / next real D1 close of the cursor symbol, not naive UTC midnight.
On weekend/closed-market, show 'mercado cerrado - reabre domingo ~22:00 UTC' instead of a countdown.
Optionally surface the cursor symbol driving the schedule.
recommendation: Front-end + a small state_reader hint (is_market_open / next_session_open). Keep it read-only; no service change.
out_of_scope: Changing the rebalance trigger logic itself (it is correct).

history.reason: feat(execution): B0104 step 5 â€” bounded equity curve (rotation + rollup)

Fixed: frontend-only fix in dashboard.html. Added market-calendar-aware countdown using IIFE: detects Sat (dow===6), Sun before 22:00 (dow===0 && hr<22), Fri after 22:00 (dow===5 && hr>=22) as closed. On closed: shows cerrado · lun ~00:00 UTC (en Xh Ym) in muted color; on open: shows normal countdown. No service restart needed. 1131 tests pass.
fix(dashboard): B0105 â€” market-calendar-aware rebalance countdown
